##NAMES=Mauriziodiniite
##RRUFFID=R250098
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(NH_4_)(As_2_O_3_)_2_I
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless and transparent hexagonal tablets, associated with calcite, cuatrocapaite-(NH_4_), lavendulan, magnesiokoritnigite and torrecillasite on matrix consisting of native arsenic, arsenolite and pyrite. Type sample, described in Kampf et al 2020
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250098
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(NH_4_)_0.94_K_0.03_(As_2_O_3_)_2_I_0.92_Cl_0.03_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.2832 b: 5.2832 c: 9.3094 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 225.03 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
